title: How Much Does Ilie Bolojan Care That Some Are Tearing Up Their Diplomas?
summary: The article criticizes Romanian PM Ilie Bolojan's decision to devalue doctoral degrees and formal education through budget cuts and symbolic messaging. The author, himself a PhD holder, argues that formal education is the foundational condition for liberal democracy and that undermining it follows the playbook of authoritarian regimes.
talking_points: Bolojan, who holds no doctorate himself, is making decisions that diminish the value of doctoral degrees while tolerating plagiarist ministers in his own government
Formal education created modern citizenship and is the prerequisite for liberal democracy - without it, democracy degenerates into ochlocracy (mob rule) and eventually dictatorship
Social media has replaced formal education as the primary paradigm-shaping force, creating fragmented realities and false expertise

Asian countries like Singapore, China, Japan, and South Korea invest heavily in education and accord high prestige to doctoral degrees, contrasting with Europe's declining respect for formal education
The decline in respect for formal education correlates with the rise of right-wing extremism in both Romania and Italy
author: Andrei Colțea
